  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section opens with a gap statement (magnitude-dependent bias in off-the-shelf classifiers) and a contribution claim (~35% average bias reduction). Reviewing requires checking whether the framing is coherent and whether the stated motivation actually leads to the described method.",
    "R1.2": "Load-bearing: multiple internal consistency issues must be checked. The headline '~35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias' is not derivable from the reported aggregate metrics (top-1 accuracy, macro-F1, AUC). No per-magnitude-bin breakdown is shown, so the quantified bias reduction figure cannot be verified from the evidence provided in this section. This is a structural logical gap.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: the central claim is bias reduction, but all reported evidence is global classification performance (accuracy, F1, AUC averaged over the full test split). These metrics do not directly measure magnitude-stratified bias. The artifact conflates improvement in aggregate metrics with reduction in a specific, stratified bias — a potential evidence-claim misalignment that is critical to flag in any review.",
    "R1.6": "A generic 'review this section' delegation reasonably includes prose quality. The section is technically coherent in structure but contains hedging constructions ('approximately 35%', 'near-uniform', 'substantially reduces') whose vagueness should be evaluated against the precision of the numeric results."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review focus on scientific correctness — specifically whether the reported aggregate metrics (accuracy, F1, AUC) are sufficient to support the magnitude-bias-reduction claims — or on writing and presentation quality only?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a gap statement (magnitude-dependent bias in off-the-shelf classifiers) and a contribution claim (~35% average bias reduction). Reviewing requires checking whether the framing is coherent and whether the stated motivation actually leads to the described method.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: multiple internal consistency issues must be checked. The headline '~35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias' is not derivable from the reported aggregate metrics (top-1 accuracy, macro-F1, AUC). No per-magnitude-bin breakdown is shown, so the quantified bias reduction figure cannot be verified from the evidence provided in this section. This is a structural logical gap.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the central claim is bias reduction, but all reported evidence is global classification performance (accuracy, F1, AUC averaged over the full test split). These metrics do not directly measure magnitude-stratified bias. The artifact conflates improvement in aggregate metrics with reduction in a specific, stratified bias — a potential evidence-claim misalignment that is critical to flag in any review.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'review this section' delegation reasonably includes prose quality. The section is technically coherent in structure but contains hedging constructions ('approximately 35%', 'near-uniform', 'substantially reduces') whose vagueness should be evaluated against the precision of the numeric results.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus on scientific correctness — specifically whether the reported aggregate metrics (accuracy, F1, AUC) are sufficient to support the magnitude-bias-reduction claims — or on writing and presentation quality only?
